tier.description: Access tier (blueprint gateway/identity: tiered access strangers vs owner).
tier.title: Tier

AnthropicProviderConfig.description:
Configuration for the `anthropic` model provider (blueprint
`model/llmrouter` — provider adapters behind one interface).

apiKeyEnv.description: Env var NAME holding the API key (never the value — contract rule 3).
apiKeyEnv.title: Apikeyenv

maxTokens.description: Max output tokens per model call (Messages API max_tokens); None = the adapter default (4096). Ceiling 128000 matches the largest current model output cap. v1 additive amendment, stage 13.
maxTokens.title: Maxtokens

pricing.description: Operator-declared token pricing for this model path; required on every selectable path iff budgets.maxUsdPerSession is set (cross-validated). None = no pricing declared. v1 additive amendment, stage 25.

AnthropicProviderConfig.title: AnthropicProviderConfig

Approval.description:
`spec.approval` — what requires human confirmation (blueprint
`capabilities/executor`, decision 'What requires human approval?').

autoApprove.description: Auto-approved tool names ('<server>.<tool>'); only meaningful with policy 'allowlist-confirm-rest'. Every entry must name a grant declared in spec.tools (cross-validated at the envelope level).
autoApprove.title: Autoapprove

policy.description: Approval policy (blueprint capabilities/executor): 'autonomous' (nothing confirmed), 'allowlist-confirm-rest' (auto-approve the allowlist, confirm the rest — default-deny), or 'everything' (everything confirmed). Default matches the blueprint's 'default-deny with a growing auto-approve list'.
policy.title: Policy

Approval.title: Approval

AuditConfig.description:
Audit sink selection inside `spec.observability` (blueprint
`persistence/auditobs`; the append-only format is factory-level, ADR 0003
item 7 — the sink location is per-agent config).

path.description: Append-only JSONL file path inside the image.
path.title: Path

sink.description: Audit sink component. Current library: jsonl.
sink.title: Sink

AuditConfig.title: AuditConfig

ChannelVerification.description:
Inbound verification for a platform channel (blueprint `gateway/identity`,
responsibility: verify webhook signatures / bot token scopes).

Names the verifying secret's env var; values never appear in the spec
(contract agent-spec, binding rule 3).

method.description: 'signature' = platform-signed payloads (webhook signing secret); 'token' = authenticated bot session (bot token); 'none' = unverified (dev only).
method.title: Method

secretEnv.description: Env var NAME holding the verifying secret (never the value).
secretEnv.title: Secretenv

ChannelVerification.title: ChannelVerification

DevHttpChannel.description:
`spec.channels[]` dev-http — localhost HTTP adapter, zero platform deps
(blueprint `channels/adapters`; transport option: webhooks — a plain HTTP
endpoint; no verification, honestly unverified).

port.description: TCP port the adapter listens on.
port.title: Port

transport.description: Delivery transport (blueprint channels/adapters): local HTTP endpoint.
transport.title: Transport

DevHttpChannel.title: DevHttpChannel

DiscordChannel.description:
`spec.channels[]` discord (blueprint `channels/adapters`; transport
options: websocket gateway (Discord) / polling (fallback)).

transport.description: Delivery transport (blueprint channels/adapters): outbound-only websocket gateway (firewall-friendly) or polling fallback.
transport.title: Transport

verification.description: Bot-token verification; names the token env var.

DiscordChannel.title: DiscordChannel

EventTrigger.description:
Event-subscription activation (ADR 0003 addition: triggers / event
subscription) — e.g. the worked example's alarm-driven outage agent.

`secretEnv` (v1 additive amendment, stage 18): the event-intake endpoint
fails closed on a shared secret, and the spec names the env var holding it
— the same spec-honesty posture as `ChannelVerification.secretEnv` (the
value is deploy-time only, never in the spec or image; contract rule 3).
Defaulted so every pre-amendment spec validates and behaves unchanged.

event.description: Event name/type filter within the source; None = all events.
event.title: Event

prompt.description: Instruction delivered with the event payload; None = payload only.
prompt.title: Prompt

secretEnv.description: Env var NAME holding the shared secret that authenticates event deliveries to the intake endpoint (never the value — contract rule 3). v1 additive amendment, stage 18.
secretEnv.title: Secretenv

source.description: Event source the agent subscribes to (e.g. 'alertmanager').
source.title: Source

EventTrigger.title: EventTrigger

FactsMemory.description:
Memory structure: structured facts — key-value / markdown files, human-
auditable (blueprint `core/memorysys`, option 'Structured facts').

store.description: No vector store (blueprint core/memorysys, decision 'Which vector store?'): facts live in the persistence tier.
store.title: Store

FactsMemory.title: FactsMemory

Gateway.description:
`spec.gateway` — the control plane: who gets in and how work is queued
(blueprint `gateway/identity` and `gateway/queue`).

allowlist.description: Access-control policy (blueprint gateway/identity). Absent = no identity layer (dev-only; the skeleton's unverified dev-http channel).

concurrency.description: Per-conversation handling (blueprint gateway/queue, decision 'Serial or concurrent handling per conversation?'): strict serial per session, or concurrent with locking.
concurrency.title: Concurrency

identityUnification.description: How one human is unified across channels (blueprint gateway/identity, decision 'How do you unify one human across channels?'): manual linking table, verification challenge, or keep identities separate.
identityUnification.title: Identityunification

queue.description: Queue weight (blueprint gateway/queue, decision 'How heavy should the queue be?'): asyncio.Queue in-process, or Redis (persistence + pub/sub).
queue.title: Queue

Gateway.title: Gateway

GatewayAllowlist.description:
Who may talk to the agent (blueprint `gateway/identity`, decision "Who is
allowed to talk to the agent?": owner-only allowlist / pairing code flow /
open with tiered permissions).

policy.description: Allowlist policy (blueprint gateway/identity): 'owner-only', 'pairing' (code flow for inviting users), or 'tiered' (open with tiered permissions).
policy.title: Policy

roster.description: Statically-declared principals; pairing may add more at runtime.
roster.title: Roster

GatewayAllowlist.title: GatewayAllowlist

HealthConfig.description:
Health-check surface (blueprint `persistence/auditobs`, responsibility:
health checks and alerting for the daemon).
HealthConfig.title: HealthConfig

HttpTransport.description:
MCP server over streamable HTTP (blueprint `capabilities/mcpmgr`,
transport option 'Streamable HTTP (remote/shared)').

url.description: Base URL of the remote MCP server.
url.title: Url

HttpTransport.title: HttpTransport

LayeredHistory.description:
History strategy: window + periodic summary + retrieval — the blueprint's
'most real systems layer them' (blueprint `core/sessions`).

summarize.description: Roll older turns into a summary.
summarize.title: Summarize

LayeredHistory.title: LayeredHistory

LayeredMemory.description:
Memory structure: facts + vectors (blueprint `core/memorysys`, option
'Layered: facts + vectors'; `corpus` scopes what gets embedded per the
memorysys retrieval responsibility).

corpus.description: What gets embedded (blueprint core/memorysys, retrieval responsibility: 'embed and search past conversations and documents'): 'agent-summaries' (agent-written summaries), 'transcripts' (raw conversation transcripts), or 'documents'. None = the structure's default corpus (transcripts).
corpus.title: Corpus

store.description: Vector store choice for the vector layer (blueprint core/memorysys): SQLite + sqlite-vec or Postgres + pgvector.
store.title: Store

LayeredMemory.title: LayeredMemory

LocalTransport.description:
Tool server backed by the runtime's in-process local tool registry
(stage-6 v1 additive amendment — a new transport enum value under contract
rule 4). No external process, no network: the grants select from the
`local_tools` component's registry of harmless demo tools, executed by the
same constraint-enforcing tool executor MCP transports plug into (stage 7).
LocalTransport.title: LocalTransport

McpServer.description:
One entry of `spec.tools` — an MCP server attached to THIS agent
(blueprint `capabilities/mcpmgr`, decision 'Which MCP servers get attached
to which agent?', option 'Per-agent allowlist'). The `spec.tools` list IS
the per-agent allowlist; anything not listed is absent from the image
(absence semantics, rule 2).

allow.description: Exhaustive positive per-tool allowlist with scopes; a server with no grants may not be attached.
allow.title: Allow

name.description: Server id, used to namespace its tools.
name.title: Name

secretEnvs.description: Env var NAMES the server needs (never values — contract rule 3); injected at runtime, never visible to the model.
secretEnvs.title: Secretenvs

transport.description: stdio child process or remote HTTP.
transport.title: Transport

McpServer.title: McpServer

Memory.description:
`spec.memory` — long-term memory (blueprint `core/memorysys`). Absent
section = no durable memory beyond the session (absence semantics, rule 2).

structure.description: Structured memory or embeddings-first (blueprint core/memorysys, decision 'Structured memory or embeddings-first?'): facts / vectors / layered.
structure.title: Structure

writePolicy.description: Who may write memory (blueprint core/memorysys considerations: 'define who may write memory — that's a trust decision'): explicit user command, the agent autonomously, or nobody (read-only). Agent-autonomous writes are privileged tool calls — always audited (blueprint core/persona considerations).
writePolicy.title: Writepolicy

Memory.title: Memory

MessageTrigger.description:
A human message activates the agent (ADR 0003 addition: triggers).

The skeleton's implicit behavior, made declarable.
MessageTrigger.title: MessageTrigger

Metadata.description: `metadata` — identity of the spec document itself.

slug.description: Kebab-case slug; the image becomes ghcr.io/<owner>/agent-keep-<slug>.
slug.title: Slug

specVersion.description: Semver of THIS document, bumped on any edit.
specVersion.title: Specversion

Metadata.title: Metadata

ModelBudgets.description:
Cost control (blueprint `model/llmrouter`, decision 'Where does cost
control live?', options 'Per-session token budgets' and 'budget alerts').

maxTokensPerSession.description: Token ceiling per session; None = unlimited.
maxTokensPerSession.title: Maxtokenspersession

maxUsdPerSession.description: USD ceiling per session; None = unlimited. Enforced against operator-declared pricing (models.*.pricing) — every selectable model path must declare pricing when this is set (cross-validated at load). v1 additive amendment, stage 25.
maxUsdPerSession.title: Maxusdpersession

onExceed.description: Exceeding a budget blocks further model calls, or warns (budget alerts).
onExceed.title: Onexceed

ModelBudgets.title: ModelBudgets

ModelTier.description:
One routing tier (blueprint `model/llmrouter`, decisions 'Route by task'
and 'Where does cost control live?', option 'Tiered routing by task type').

anthropic.description: Anthropic provider config; required iff provider is 'anthropic'.

static.description: Static provider config; required iff provider is 'static'.

ModelTier.title: ModelTier

Models.description:
`spec.models` — model routing, providers, and budgets (blueprint
`model/llmrouter`). 'static' is a first-class provider (ADR 0004).

anthropic.description: Anthropic provider config; required iff provider is 'anthropic'.

budgets.description: Per-session cost control (blueprint model/llmrouter); None = no budgets.

provider.description: Default provider when no tier routing applies (blueprint model/llmrouter).
provider.title: Provider

static.description: Static provider config; required iff provider is 'static'.

tiers.description: Routing tiers by task type (blueprint model/llmrouter: cheap model for triage, flagship for reasoning). Empty = single default provider.
tiers.title: Tiers

Models.title: Models

health.description: Health-check declaration; None = the channel adapter's default surface.

Persistence.description:
`spec.persistence` — storage tier for sessions/transcripts/vectors
(blueprint `persistence/stores`, decision 'Files, SQLite, or Postgres?').

tier.description: Storage tier (blueprint persistence/stores): 'files' (JSONL + markdown), 'sqlite', or 'postgres'. You can graduate through all three.
tier.title: Tier

Persistence.title: Persistence

Persona.description:
`spec.persona` — identity, tone, standing instructions, and where
personalization lives (blueprint `core/persona`, decision "Where does
personalization live?": static config / learned memory / both with clear
precedence).

instructions.description: Standing instructions, merged deterministically after identity and tone.
instructions.title: Instructions

precedence.description: Conflict rule when source is 'both' (blueprint core/persona: 'Both, with clear precedence'). Required iff source is 'both'.
precedence.title: Precedence

source.description: Where personalization lives (blueprint core/persona): 'static' config in this spec, 'learned' memory the agent updates, or 'both'. Learned-persona writes are privileged, audited memory writes (see spec.memory.writePolicy).
source.title: Source

tone.description: Voice/tone guidance merged into the system prompt.
tone.title: Tone

Persona.title: Persona

Pricing.description:
Operator-declared token pricing for one model path (blueprint
`model/llmrouter`, decision 'Where does cost control live?').

There is NO library price table: prices drift and a stale table silently
mis-enforces, so the OPERATOR declares pricing in the spec, right next to
the model it prices, and `budgets.maxUsdPerSession` enforces against these
exact numbers (honest, auditable, no hidden knobs — ADR 0003 spirit).
Both rates are required together (both-or-neither): omit the `pricing`
block entirely for no pricing. v1 additive amendment, stage 25.

usdPerMillionInputTokens.description: USD charged per 1,000,000 input (prompt) tokens for this model path.
usdPerMillionInputTokens.title: Usdpermillioninputtokens

usdPerMillionOutputTokens.description: USD charged per 1,000,000 output (completion) tokens for this model path.
usdPerMillionOutputTokens.title: Usdpermillionoutputtokens

Pricing.title: Pricing

RetrievalHistory.description: History strategy: retrieval of relevant past turns (blueprint `core/sessions`).
RetrievalHistory.title: RetrievalHistory

Sandbox.description:
`spec.sandbox` — execution isolation + network egress (blueprint
`capabilities/executor`, decision 'How isolated is code/shell execution?';
egress is ADR 0003's second addition).

v1 clarification note: `egress` entries were an unconstrained `list[str]`
in the stage-1 schema. Host[:port] format validation was added in stage 2,
while the field had exactly one consumer (the walking skeleton's empty
list), as a format clarification of the always-intended value space — a
network egress allowlist of hosts — not a v1 break.

egress.description: Network egress allowlist of host[:port] entries (ADR 0003 addition: egress). Exhaustive positive declaration; default EMPTY — nothing else is reachable by construction.
egress.title: Egress

profile.description: Tool-execution isolation (blueprint capabilities/executor): 'same-process' (same process/user — the blueprint says don't), 'restricted-user' (dedicated unprivileged user), or 'container' (container/VM per execution). Default container — every built agent is already a container (ADR 0003 item 8).
profile.title: Profile

Sandbox.title: Sandbox

ScheduleTrigger.description:
Cron-scheduled activation (ADR 0003 addition: triggers / schedule).

v1 clarification note (#10, stage 22 — the stage-2/5 format-clarification
procedure under contract rule 4): the original `cron` pattern validated
token COUNT only, so `never gonna give you up` passed. Real five-field
validation (`keep_spec.cron.parse_cron`) pins the always-intended value
space — an executable cron expression — now that the schedule trigger has
a runtime component. Every in-repo spec's cron string was already valid.

cron.description: Five-field cron expression (minute hour day-of-month month day-of-week), evaluated in UTC. Per field: '*', numbers, ranges (N-M), comma lists, and '/step' on '*' or a range; day-of-week 0-7 (0 and 7 both Sunday). Exactly five fields — @names are not supported.
cron.title: Cron

prompt.description: Instruction delivered to the agent when the schedule fires.
prompt.title: Prompt

ScheduleTrigger.title: ScheduleTrigger

definition.description: What a session IS (blueprint core/sessions, decision 'What is a session, exactly?'): per channel-conversation, per user (shared across channels), or hybrid (shared memory, separate transcripts). Absent = the skeleton's single session.
definition.title: Definition

history.description: How history fits the context window (blueprint core/sessions, decision 'How do you fit history into the context window?'): sliding-window / summarization / retrieval / layered. Absent = whole-session history (skeleton).
history.title: History

mode.description: Stage-1 session manager component. Skeleton subset: single.
mode.title: Mode

SkillPack.description:
One entry of `spec.skills` — an instruction pack (knowledge, never code;
factory-level ADR 0003 item 5 fixes the skill definition) with its selection
strategy (blueprint `capabilities/skills`, decision 'How are skills selected
per request?').

keywords.description: Trigger keywords; required iff selection is 'keyword'.
keywords.title: Keywords

name.description: Skill pack id in the skill registry.
name.title: Name

selection.description: Selection strategy (blueprint capabilities/skills): 'always' in prompt, 'keyword' (keyword/intent triggering), or 'model-driven' (descriptions in prompt, bodies on demand).
selection.title: Selection

version.description: Version pin (blueprint capabilities/skills: pin what production agents use).
version.title: Version

SkillPack.title: SkillPack

SlackChannel.description:
`spec.channels[]` slack (blueprint `channels/adapters`; transport
options: webhooks (Slack) / websocket (Socket Mode) / polling (fallback)).

transport.description: Delivery transport (blueprint channels/adapters): webhook (Events API, needs a public HTTPS endpoint), websocket (Socket Mode), or polling fallback.
transport.title: Transport

verification.description: Request-signature verification; names the signing-secret env var.

SlackChannel.title: SlackChannel

maxTurns.description: Turns kept before the oldest is dropped.
maxTurns.title: Maxturns

SmsChannel.description:
`spec.channels[]` sms (blueprint `channels/adapters`; transport options:
webhooks (SMS gateway callback) / polling (fallback)).

verification.description: Gateway-signature verification; names the secret env var.

SmsChannel.title: SmsChannel

SpecSections.description:
`spec` — the agent-level sections (contract agent-spec schema block).

Sections required by the walking skeleton stay required; every section and
field added since stage 1 is optional or defaulted (additive, rule 4).

memory.description: Long-term memory; absent = none built (absence semantics).

skills.description: Instruction packs enabled for this agent; absent tools/skills are absent.
skills.title: Skills

tools.description: Per-agent MCP server allowlist; empty = no external tools in the image.
tools.title: Tools

triggers.description: What activates the agent; absent = message-only.

SpecSections.title: SpecSections

StaticProviderConfig.description:
Configuration for the `static` model provider (ADR 0004 — deterministic,
hermetic, first-class).

script.description: Deterministic scripted replies; call N returns script[N % len(script)].
script.title: Script

StaticProviderConfig.title: StaticProviderConfig

StdioTransport.description:
MCP server as a local child process (blueprint `capabilities/mcpmgr`,
transport option 'stdio'; the factory supports both transports behind one
interface — ADR 0003 item 4).
StdioTransport.title: StdioTransport

summarizeAfterTurns.description: Turns accumulated before a summarization pass.
summarizeAfterTurns.title: Summarizeafterturns

ToolGrant.description:
One allowed tool on an MCP server (blueprint `capabilities/mcpmgr`:
'tool availability IS the permission model').

`constraints` (v1 additive amendment, stage 4) narrows a grant further:
hard parameter pins the runtime tool executor MUST enforce (e.g.
`room: noc-outages` on a paging tool). No constraint-enforcing executor
exists yet, so any grant carrying constraints fails the buildable-check
loudly (same fail-loud pattern as egress/approval).

constraints.description: Hard parameter pins the tool executor MUST enforce: constraint name -> validated scalar (identifier-ish string, int, or bool — never free prose), e.g. pinning a paging tool to `room: noc-outages`. None = no pins.
constraints.title: Constraints

name.description: Tool name as exposed by the MCP server.
name.title: Name

scope.description: Grant scope (blueprint capabilities/mcpmgr considerations: 'the public-facing agent gets read-only tools'). Default read-only — least privilege.
scope.title: Scope

ToolGrant.title: ToolGrant

Triggers.description:
`spec.triggers` — what activates the agent (ADR 0003 addition:
message / schedule / event subscription). Absent section = message-only.

activations.description: Exhaustive positive list of activations; default is message-only.
activations.title: Activations

Triggers.title: Triggers

VectorMemory.description:
Memory structure: vector store over transcripts (blueprint
`core/memorysys`, option 'Vector store over transcripts'; `corpus` scopes
what gets embedded per the memorysys retrieval responsibility).

corpus.description: What gets embedded (blueprint core/memorysys, retrieval responsibility: 'embed and search past conversations and documents'): 'agent-summaries' (agent-written summaries), 'transcripts' (raw conversation transcripts), or 'documents'. None = the structure's default corpus (transcripts).
corpus.title: Corpus

store.description: Vector store choice (blueprint core/memorysys, decision 'Which vector store, when you get there?'): SQLite + sqlite-vec or Postgres + pgvector.
store.title: Store

VectorMemory.title: VectorMemory

verification.description: Webhook-signature verification; names the secret env var.
